name: Forged Identity Document
description: A government-issue ID card with an altered photograph and a different name. The laminate is slightly bubbled near the photo.
incidents: border crossing flag at gate 3
bank account opened under false name
police stop — officer noted inconsistency

name: Unlicensed Handgun
description: A small semi-automatic pistol with the serial number filed off. One round in the chamber. No registered owner.
incidents: recovered near scene of robbery
fingerprints matched to two prior incidents
ballistics flagged in unsolved case

name: Stolen Access Card — Restricted Zone
description: An electronic keycard for a restricted government facility. Reported stolen three weeks ago. Still active.
incidents: used twice after reported stolen
door log shows entry to server room
facility raised alert after second use

name: Prescription Medication — No Documentation
description: A bottle of 40mg oxycodone tablets. Label removed. No prescription slip present. Over the legal carry limit.
incidents: similar batch linked to two overdoses
hospital reported missing stock
intercepted in three other stops this month

name: Wiretapping Device
description: A small audio transmitter disguised as a plug socket adapter. Broadcasts on an unregistered frequency.
incidents: matching device found in council chambers
used in prior corporate espionage case
frequency linked to known illegal broadcast network

name: Counterfeit Currency — Uncut Sheet
description: An uncut sheet of high-quality counterfeit banknotes. Print quality is near-perfect. Fails UV check only.
incidents: same print batch flagged at three banks
used to pay a supplier — spotted on deposit
watermark pattern matches known forgery network

name: Stolen Laptop — Police Evidence Tag Still Attached
description: A laptop with an evidence sticker from a 2021 fraud investigation. Hard drive not wiped. Case still open.
incidents: reported missing from evidence log in March
IT flag on active investigation files
chain of custody broken — internal inquiry opened

name: Unregistered Burner Phone Array
description: Seven prepaid SIM cards and three handsets, all unregistered. Call logs show encrypted messaging only.
incidents: number linked to extortion message
two numbers active during known drug delivery window
IMEI flagged in prior trafficking case

name: Classified Government Report — Stamped RESTRICTED
description: A 14-page internal report on infrastructure vulnerabilities. Stamped RESTRICTED. Not authorised for public or private distribution.
incidents: document reported missing from department six months ago
partial contents published anonymously online
two civil servants under investigation

name: Knife Above Legal Length
description: A fixed-blade knife with a 14cm blade. Exceeds the legal 3-inch carry limit. No hunting or occupational permit found.
incidents: similar blade used in assault last month
vendor linked to two prior seizures
blade pattern matches description from witness statement

name: Forged Work Permit
description: A work authorisation permit for a restricted trade. The issuing officer stamp does not match any registered officer.
incidents: same issuer stamp on four other forgeries seized this year
employer unaware permit was fake
holder gained access to secure site on strength of document

name: Lock Picks — Full Set
description: A professional-grade set of 22 picks and tension wrenches in a leather wallet. Ownership requires a locksmith licence. None found.
incidents: type of pick matches marks at three recent break-ins
set sold by supplier under investigation
no licence registered to suspect or known associates

name: Police Radio — Reprogrammed
description: A decommissioned police radio reprogrammed to receive active officer channels. Receiving only, not transmitting.
incidents: active channel compromise flagged by comms team
similar device found during drug raid last quarter
channel used was live during two operations

name: Bag of Unmarked Cash — Large Denomination
description: Just under £10,000 in used, non-sequential banknotes. Amount falls just below the mandatory reporting threshold.
incidents: serial numbers overlap with notes seized in prior case
amount consistent with known payment schedule
found alongside financial contact of known criminal

name: Explosive Precursor Chemicals
description: Two sealed containers of ammonium nitrate and a small bottle of nitromethane. Legal in industrial contexts — no industrial licence present.
incidents: supplier flagged by counter-terrorism unit
combination matches formula in intercepted message
quantities consistent with device construction

name: Stolen Postal Package — Still Sealed
description: A sealed parcel addressed to a government agency. Postmark three weeks ago. No explanation for possession.
incidents: contents listed as sensitive equipment on manifest
sender reported interception to postal authority
two other packages from same route also missing
